text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION: 59-year-old female with history of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, status post neoadjuvant mFOLFIRINOX x8 cycles, consolidative chemoradiation (IMRT 50.4 Gy with concurrent cape), and subsequent pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with R0 resection (ypT2 ypN2 M0). Patient then developed biopsy-confirmed hepatic metastases during adjuvant Gemzar/cape and was transitioned to second-line nanoliposomal iri (nal-IRI) plus 5-FU/leucovorin. This scan is obtained after 4 cycles of nal-IRI-based therapy for disease reassessment. Rising CA 19-9. Evaluate for response versus progression.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis acquired in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of iohexol intravenous contrast. Coronal and sagittal reformations reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT abdomen/pelvis obtained at initiation of second-line therapy and to MRI abdomen with hepatobiliary protocol obtained prior to liver biopsy.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidation identified. No pleural effusions. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. No endobronchial lesions.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. No pericardial effusion. Heart size is within normal limits for technique.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified in the visualized thoracic skeleton. No rib erosion or cortical destruction.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is within normal limits in size. There has been interval marked progression of hepatic metastatic disease since the prior MRI and CT at the time of biopsy.

- Segment VI (right lobe): A hypodense lesion previously measuring 9 mm on the hepatobiliary-protocol MRI now measures approximately 2.1 cm in greatest axial dimension. The lesion demonstrates peripheral rim hypoenhancement relative to the background hepatic parenchyma and is poorly marginated with subtle mass effect on adjacent parenchyma. This represents substantial interval enlargement.

- Segment VII (right lobe): A hypodense lesion previously measuring 7 mm now measures approximately 1.6 cm. Similar morphology to the segment VI lesion with peripheral hypoenhancement and ill-defined margins. The lesion abuts the hepatic capsule posteriorly without definite capsular breakthrough.

- Segment IV (medial left lobe, approaching the falciform ligament): A newly identified hypodense lesion measuring approximately 1.2 cm. Not seen on prior CT or MRI studies. The lesion has a rounded configuration with mild peripheral enhancement and hypodense central core on this single-phase acquisition.

- Segment VIII (right lobe, subcapsular): An additional new hypodense lesion measuring approximately 0.8 cm in the right hepatic lobe, subcapsular in location. This lesion was not present on prior imaging.

In aggregate, there are now four hepatic lesions identified (two previously characterized by biopsy-confirmed metastatic adenocarcinoma, and two new lesions), all demonstrating imaging morphology consistent with hepatic metastases in this clinical setting. No evidence of central hepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The hepatic veins, portal vein, and hepatic arteries are patent without thrombosis. No perihepatic ascites at this time.

Gallbladder: The patient is status post cholecystectomy as part of the prior Whipple procedure. Surgical clips are seen in the gallbladder fossa. No biloma.

Bile Ducts: The common hepatic duct and visualized intrahepatic bile ducts appear within normal limits in caliber, appropriate given the prior hepaticojejunostomy reconstruction. The biliary stent from the pre-neoadjuvant ERCP is no longer in situ (as expected given surgical resection). No biliary obstruction.

Pancreas: The pancreatic head and uncinate process are absent, consistent with prior pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy. The remnant pancreatic body and tail are preserved; no focal mass or ductal dilatation is identified in the remnant pancreas. Surgical clips and changes are present in the surgical bed. No discrete fluid collections or abscess in the operative bed. No evidence of local anastomotic recurrence.

Spleen: Unremarkable. No splenic lesions.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands appear unremarkable. No adrenal lesions or adrenal enlargement.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size, contour, and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. No urolithiasis identified.

Stomach and Duodenum: The stomach is unremarkable. The patient is status post duodenojejunostomy reconstruction; the anastomosis appears patent without luminal narrowing or obstruction. No wall thickening or mucosal irregularity in the visualized stomach.

Small Bowel and Colon: The Roux-en-Y hepaticojejunostomy and pancreaticojejunostomy limbs appear patent. No small bowel obstruction. No evidence of perforation. The colon appears unremarkable. No colonic wall thickening or masses.

Peritoneum and Mesentery: No peritoneal nodules, implants, or thickening to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis. The mesentery appears within normal limits without discrete soft tissue deposits. No omental caking. No ascites in the abdomen at this time.

Vascular Structures: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery are patent. The portal vein and superior mesenteric vein are patent. No vascular thrombosis.

Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or para-aortic lymph nodes. No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder demonstrates normal wall thickness and no intraluminal filling defects.

Reproductive Organs: The uterus and bilateral ovaries appear unremarkable without masses.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No enlarged inguinal or pelvic lymph nodes.

Osseous Structures (Abdomen and Pelvis): Visualized lumbar spine and pelvic bony structures demonstrate no suspicious sclerotic or lytic lesions. No pathologic fracture. Degenerative changes are present in the lower lumbar spine, compatible with the patient's age.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Hepatic metastases - interval significant progression. Four hepatic lesions are now identified, representing two previously biopsy-confirmed metastatic deposits that have grown substantially (segment VI: 9 mm to 2.1 cm; segment VII: 7 mm to 1.6 cm) and two new hepatic lesions in segments IV (1.2 cm) and VIII (0.8 cm) not present on prior imaging. The overall hepatic disease burden has increased markedly since the prior studies obtained prior to initiation of second-line therapy.

2. No evidence of locoregional recurrence in the Whipple surgical bed.

3. No peritoneal metastases or ascites identified on this examination.

4. No pulmonary metastases or thoracic disease.

5. No osseous metastatic disease in the visualized skeleton.

In the clinical context of rising CA 19-9 (312 U/mL at time of this scan, compared to 58 U/mL at initiation of second-line therapy), the imaging findings are consistent with disease progression on nanoliposomal iri-based second-line chemotherapy.
